info.description: HTTP API served by `contenox serve` under /api. Generated from route annotations by tools/openapi-gen.
info.title: Contenox Runtime API

parameters.description: An optional RFC3339Nano timestamp to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: list returns the declared agents in the registry, paginated by cursor.

parameters.description: The unique name of the agent.
parameters.name: name

get.summary: getByName returns one declared agent by its unique name.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the agent.
parameters.name: id

get.summary: get returns one declared agent by ID.

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: list returns pending approvals, newest first — the inbox itself.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the approval.
parameters.name: id

post.summary: answer resolves a pending approval.

parameters.description: An optional RFC3339Nano timestamp to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: listBackends returns the registered backends, each merged with its observed runtime state (downloaded models, pull progress, last error).

post.summary: createBackend registers a new inference backend and returns it with its assigned ID.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the backend.
parameters.name: id

delete.summary: deleteBackend removes a backend registration by ID.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the backend.
parameters.name: id

get.summary: getBackend returns one backend by ID, merged with its observed runtime state (downloaded models, pull progress, last error).

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the backend.
parameters.name: id

put.summary: updateBackend replaces a backend's stored configuration by ID and returns the updated record.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the backend.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The model name to store the pushed artifact under.
parameters.name: name

requestBody.description: Raw model file bytes (GGUF or OpenVINO artifact), streamed as the request body.

post.summary: pushModel streams a GGUF file straight from the request body to the models store of a specific modeld backend (local or remote) — the HTTP twin of `contenox model push`.

get.summary: getCLIConfig returns the resolved CLI configuration snapshot (default model, provider, chain, and related settings).

put.summary: putCLIConfig updates the provided CLI configuration keys and returns the resolved snapshot after the change.

parameters.description: File path relative to the project root.
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.
parameters.name: root

parameters.description: Tree view to apply: 'full' (default, raw tree) or 'agent' (the tree as the agent sees it, with per-path access verdicts).
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Directory path relative to the project root.
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: HITL policy name to evaluate the agent view against; omitted uses the configured default.
parameters.name: policy

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.
parameters.name: root

get.summary: list returns the directory entries under path, optionally filtered to a server-side tree view (e.g.

post.summary: createFile writes a new file from the request payload and returns its entry.

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.
parameters.name: root

put.summary: updateFile overwrites an existing file from the request payload and returns its entry.

parameters.description: File path relative to the project root.
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.

get.summary: content returns a file's contents with metadata, UTF-8 inline or base64 for binary data.

parameters.description: File path relative to the project root.
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.
parameters.name: root

200.description: The file's raw bytes, served with its stored content type (application/octet-stream fallback).

get.summary: download serves a file's raw bytes with its stored content type.

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.
parameters.name: root

put.summary: movePath renames or moves a file or folder and returns the entry at its new path.

parameters.description: Path relative to the project root.
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.
parameters.name: root

get.summary: stat returns the metadata entry for one path.
list returns every fleet instance the kernel currently tracks.

post.summary: dispatch allocates a unit via fleetservice.Dispatch and returns 202 with the ids as soon as the session is open; the orchestration (Enabled check, teardown-on-failure, the mission record, the detached first turn) lives entirely in the service now.

get.summary: list returns the observed-presence entries: external agents (and serve itself) that self-registered into the shared presence store.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the instance.
parameters.name: instanceID

delete.summary: stop tears an instance down via fleetservice.Stop, which is idempotent by kernel contract: stopping an unknown or already-stopped instance is a no-op 200, not a 404 — mirrors the delete-response idiom missionapi/mcpserverapi use (a plain "deleted" string body).

parameters.description: The unique ID of the instance.
parameters.name: instanceID

get.summary: get returns the live status of one fleet instance.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the instance.
parameters.name: instanceID

post.summary: cancel cancels an in-flight prompt turn via fleetservice.Cancel.

parameters.description: Folder path relative to the project root.
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.
parameters.name: root

parameters.description: Workspace root the request operates in: a granted root (or a directory under one); empty or "/" resolves to the default (first-configured) root.
parameters.name: root

post.summary: createFolder creates a directory and returns its entry.

get.summary: GET /health

parameters.description: Policy filename, e.g. hitl-policy-custom.json.
parameters.name: name

delete.summary: deletePolicy removes a HITL policy file by name.

parameters.description: Policy filename, e.g. hitl-policy-strict.json.
parameters.name: name

get.summary: getPolicy reads one HITL policy file by name.

parameters.description: Policy filename, e.g. hitl-policy-custom.json.
parameters.name: name

post.summary: createPolicy writes a new HITL policy file and returns the stored policy.

parameters.description: Policy filename, e.g. hitl-policy-strict.json.
parameters.name: name

put.summary: updatePolicy overwrites an existing HITL policy file and returns the stored policy.

get.summary: listPolicies returns the file names of every stored HITL policy (hitl-policy*.json).

parameters.description: An optional RFC3339Nano timestamp to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: list returns the registered MCP servers, paginated by cursor.

post.summary: create registers an MCP server and returns it with its assigned ID.

parameters.description: The unique name of the MCP server.
parameters.name: name

get.summary: getByName returns one MCP server by its unique name.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the MCP server.
parameters.name: id

delete.summary: delete removes an MCP server registration by ID.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the MCP server.
parameters.name: id

get.summary: get returns one MCP server by ID.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the MCP server.
parameters.name: id

put.summary: update replaces an MCP server's configuration by ID and returns the stored result.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the MCP server.
parameters.name: id

post.summary: oauthStart begins the OAuth authorization flow for an MCP server and returns the provider authorization URL to visit.

parameters.description: The OAuth authorization code returned by the provider.
parameters.name: code

parameters.description: The provider error code, when authorization fails.
parameters.name: error

parameters.description: The provider error description, when authorization fails.
parameters.name: error_description

parameters.description: The OAuth state value returned by the provider.
parameters.name: state

302.description: 302 redirect back to the client UI with success or error query parameters appended; no response body.

get.summary: oauthCallback completes an MCP server OAuth flow from the provider's redirect and 302-redirects back to the client UI.

parameters.description: An optional RFC3339Nano timestamp to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: list returns mission records newest-first, keyset-paginated by creation time.

post.summary: create stores a new mission record and returns the created mission.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the mission.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The unique ID of the mission.
parameters.name: id

get.summary: get returns one mission record whole.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the mission.
parameters.name: id

patch.summary: patch updates a mission's intent and/or status, and can bind it to a session and instance in the same call.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the mission.
parameters.name: id

get.summary: changes returns mission id's changed-files list (ordered by Degree-of-Interest) and scope summary.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the mission.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The changed file path to diff.
parameters.name: path

get.summary: diff returns the {original, modified} pair for one changed path in mission id, selected by the required `path` query parameter.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the mission.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: listReports returns mission id's reports newest-first.

parameters.description: The unique ID of the mission.
parameters.name: id

post.summary: addReport files a report against mission id.

get.summary: list returns the registered models as descriptors, paginated by cursor.

post.summary: create adds a model registry entry and returns it with its assigned ID.
download fetches a registered model's artifact to the local model store, reporting whether it was already present.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the model registry entry.
parameters.name: id

delete.summary: delete removes a model registry entry by ID.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the model registry entry.
parameters.name: id

get.summary: get returns one model registry entry by ID.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the model registry entry.
parameters.name: id

put.summary: update replaces a model registry entry by ID and returns the stored result.

parameters.description: Registered local model name or id to describe.
parameters.name: model

get.summary: capacity describes how a registered local model would run on this host (planned context length, memory fit) without loading it.

post.summary: load loads a registered local model into the modeld slot and returns the resulting active-model state.

get.summary: models lists the local models known to the registry state.
status reports the modeld daemon's detected state (binary, endpoint, protocol) and, when reachable, its current slot occupancy.

post.summary: unload evicts the currently loaded model from the modeld slot.

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: listInternal returns the models currently observed on the runtime's backends, with per-model capability flags.

post.summary: handle serves an OpenAI-compatible chat completion, answering with a single JSON completion object or, when the request sets stream, the same content as SSE chat.completion.chunk frames over text/event-stream.

handle serves an OpenAI-compatible (FIM) text completion, answering with a single JSON text_completion object or, when the request sets stream, the same content as SSE chunk frames over text/event-stream.

handle serves an OpenAI-compatible (FIM) text completion, answering with a single JSON text_completion object or, when the request sets stream, the same content as SSE chunk frames over text/event-stream.

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: listModels returns the models observed across the runtime's backends in the OpenAI-compatible model-list shape.

post.summary: handle serves an OpenAI-compatible chat completion, answering with a single JSON completion object or, when the request sets stream, the same content as SSE chat.completion.chunk frames over text/event-stream.

handle serves an OpenAI-compatible (FIM) text completion, answering with a single JSON text_completion object or, when the request sets stream, the same content as SSE chunk frames over text/event-stream.

parameters.description: The ID of the chain that links to the openAI completion API. Currently unused.
parameters.name: chainID

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: listModels returns the models observed across the runtime's backends in the OpenAI-compatible model-list shape.

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: list returns the operator inbox newest-first: reports that reached no live supervisor.

parameters.description: An optional RFC3339Nano timestamp to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: listConfigs returns the status of every configured provider.
supported lists the provider types this runtime can be configured with and their capabilities.

parameters.description: Provider type to delete.
parameters.name: providerType

delete.summary: deleteConfig removes a provider type's stored configuration.

parameters.description: Provider type to retrieve.
parameters.name: providerType

get.summary: get returns the stored configuration status for one provider type.

parameters.description: Provider type to configure.
parameters.name: providerType

post.summary: configure stores (or updates) a cloud provider's API key configuration and returns the resulting provider status.

parameters.description: Provider type to inspect.
parameters.name: providerType

get.summary: status reports whether a provider type is configured and ready.
getStatus reports the cached setup-check result: which pieces of the local runtime are configured and working.

post.summary: refreshStatus re-runs the setup checks and returns the fresh result.

get.summary: list returns the observed runtime state of every registered backend, with each backend's model list normalized to observed model names.

parameters.description: Task request ID to subscribe to.
parameters.name: requestId

200.description: SSE stream over text/event-stream: each data frame is one taskengine.TaskEvent JSON object for the subscribed requestId.

get.summary: stream subscribes the caller to a Server-Sent Events stream of TaskEvent objects.

parameters.description: Relative chain JSON path inside .contenox.
parameters.name: path

delete.summary: deleteTaskChain removes a task-chain definition file by path.

parameters.description: Relative chain JSON path inside .contenox.
parameters.name: path

get.summary: getTaskChain reads one task-chain definition from its file path (query parameter `path`).

parameters.description: Relative chain JSON path inside .contenox.
parameters.name: path

post.summary: createTaskChain writes a new task-chain definition file and returns the stored definition.

parameters.description: Relative chain JSON path inside .contenox.
parameters.name: path

put.summary: updateTaskChain overwrites an existing task-chain definition file and returns the stored definition.

get.summary: listTaskChains returns the relative file paths of every stored task-chain definition.

post.summary: execute runs the submitted task chain through the configured agent and returns the output together with the captured per-step state and stop reason.

parameters.description: An optional RFC3339Nano timestamp to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Serve credential accepted as a query-parameter auth fallback (read via principalFromRequest); auth headers take precedence when present.
parameters.name: token

parameters.description: Serve credential accepted as a query-parameter auth fallback (read via principalFromRequest); auth headers take precedence when present.
parameters.name: token

post.summary: createSession opens a new host terminal session for the authenticated principal and returns its ID plus the websocket attach URL.

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the terminal session.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Serve credential accepted as a query-parameter auth fallback (read via principalFromRequest); auth headers take precedence when present.
parameters.name: token

204.description: session closed; the handler writes 204 with no body

delete.summary: deleteSession closes a terminal session by ID and responds 204.

parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Serve credential accepted as a query-parameter auth fallback (read via principalFromRequest); auth headers take precedence when present.
parameters.name: token

get.summary: getSession returns one of the principal's terminal sessions by ID.
listLocal returns every locally available tool group (builtin, MCP-backed, and remote) with the tools each currently exposes.

parameters.description: An optional RFC3339Nano timestamp to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: list returns the registered remote tools providers, paginated by cursor.

post.summary: create registers a new remote tools provider and returns it with its assigned ID.

parameters.description: The unique name for the remote tool.
parameters.name: name

get.summary: getByName returns one remote tools provider by its unique name.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the remote tool.
parameters.name: id

delete.summary: delete removes a remote tools provider by ID.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the remote tool.
parameters.name: id

get.summary: get returns one remote tools provider by ID.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the remote tool.
parameters.name: id

put.summary: update replaces a remote tools provider's configuration by ID and returns the stored result.

get.summary: getSchemas returns the OpenAPI schema document for every supported tool, keyed by tool name.
GET /version

parameters.description: Set to "agent" to annotate each match with the agent's per-path access verdict; empty or "full" streams raw entries.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Filename match pattern(s), comma-separated; filepath.Match semantics against the basename, or the root-relative path when a pattern contains '/'. A file matches if ANY pattern matches (e.g. "*.md" or "*.go,*.md").
parameters.name: glob

parameters.description: Maximum matches to stream before truncating; capped at the server maximum.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Directory to search under, relative to the workspace root; defaults to the whole workspace.
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: HITL policy the agent view is evaluated against; empty uses the configured default. Ignored unless filter=agent.
parameters.name: policy

parameters.description: Workspace root to search; empty or "/" uses the configured default. Must be an allowlisted root or a directory under one.
parameters.name: root

200.description: Named SSE events over text/event-stream: `match` frames (one localfileapi.Entry JSON object each), then a terminal `done` frame carrying totals and the truncation flag.

get.summary: find streams the file entries under `root` whose names match `glob` as SSE.

parameters.description: Workspace root directory to revoke.
parameters.name: path

get.summary: GET /workspace/roots

post.summary: POST /workspace/roots

parameters.description: Maximum matches to stream before truncating; capped at the server maximum.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Search query, matched as a literal substring (smart-case).
parameters.name: q

parameters.description: Workspace root to search; empty or "/" uses the configured default. Must be an allowlisted root or a directory under one.
parameters.name: root

200.description: Named SSE events over text/event-stream: `match` frames (one searchMatch JSON object each), then a terminal `done` frame carrying totals and the truncation flag.

get.summary: search streams matches for `q` under `root` as Server-Sent Events.
